medications.indication_rationale: Apply to lesions and beyond the border; continue past clearance.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply twice daily to and beyond the border (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: hydrocortisone 1% (mild topical steroid) over the antifungal — 3 days only
medications.indication_rationale: Brief course to rapidly reduce intense itch/inflammation and help the excoriations heal; limit to ~3 days to avoid masking tinea.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply over the antifungal twice daily for 3 days only (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Systemic itch relief to break the itch-scratch cycle; confirm paediatric dose.
medications.dose_route_frequency: paediatric dose per source note (e.g., 5 mg) — confirm for age/weight

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Keep nails short, apply antifungal beyond the ring's edge, wash hands and scrub nails, and don't share towels/bedding.

patient_education_points.point: This is ringworm — a common soil fungus that grows outward in a ring. The antifungal cream kills it; apply it a couple of centimetres past the red edge, and keep going for a week after it looks clear.
To stop the scratching and bleeding, use the mild steroid over the antifungal for just 3 days, give the anti-itch medicine, and keep the nails short. Don't share towels/bedding.
If a ring becomes hot, swollen, oozes thick pus, or your child develops a fever — go to the emergency department.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Clearing of the fungal infection and healing of the excoriations.

must_include_items: Diagnose tinea corporis and screen for cellulitis/impetigo
Topical antifungal past the border + brief (3-day) mild steroid + antihistamine + short nails
acceptable_alternatives: Short oral antifungal for extensive disease
errors_of_commission: Treating as cellulitis (ED/antibiotics) without red flags
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires antifungal (past the border) + itch-cycle control + hygiene, with a secondary-infection safety-net.
